description_long: This is a Technics-style record player with a sleek, rectangular black chassis supported by four sturdy feet. The large, circular turntable platter dominates the top surface, featuring a textured, grooved edge and a central spindle for holding vinyl records. A metallic tonearm with a blue accent is mounted to the right, curving gracefully over the platter and terminating in a small cartridge and stylus assembly. Several control knobs, switches, and sliders are distributed along the front and right edges, including a prominent start/stop button, pitch control slider, and rotary knobs. The surface is mostly glossy black with metallic and plastic details, and the branding is visible near the lower right corner. No dust cover is present, leaving all components exposed and easily accessible for operation.
description: A glossy black Technics-style record player with a large grooved platter, metallic tonearm, and multiple control knobs and sliders. The device is compact, sturdy, and designed for manual vinyl playback.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the record player, the platter, tonearm, and main control buttons and branding.
description_view_1: Right-side view highlighting the pitch control slider, tonearm base, and additional control knobs.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the back of the tonearm, platter, and rear control features.
description_view_3: Left-side view emphasizing the start/stop button, platter edge, and the left side of the tonearm.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present and occupies the central top area of the record player. It is large, circular, and features a textured, grooved edge for grip and stability of vinyl records. The platter is primarily black with a metallic, dotted ring pattern around the edge, and a central spindle is visible. The surface appears to be made of metal with a rubberized mat for record protection, and the finish is semi-glossy.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, metallic, and mounted on the right side of the player. It is straight with a gentle curve near the cartridge end, medium in length, and features a blue accent at the headshell. The tonearm is resting in its cradle, with visible joints and a counterweight at the rear. The finish is metallic silver with a smooth, slightly glossy appearance.
attribute_stylus: The stylus is visible, attached to the end of the tonearm within the cartridge assembly. It is small, pointed, and likely made of metal and plastic, appearing as a distinct component at the tip of the tonearm.
attribute_control_knobs: Multiple control knobs, switches, and buttons are present. There is a large round start/stop button on the front left, a pitch control slider on the right, and several smaller rotary knobs and switches along the front and right edges. The controls are primarily black or silver, with some colored accents (red, blue), and are made of plastic or metal. Some controls have visible labels and markings.
